verses.text: What in the leisured classes, or those busied in affairs?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: What in profane schools of philosophy or in our own?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: There is one, which runs through all, and is the greatest—his writings and labours.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nor do writers require any supply of matter besides his teaching or writings.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: All the laborious studies of old days in the Divine oracles are silent, while the new ones are in everybody’s mouth, and he is the best teacher among us who has the deepest acquaintance with his works, and speaks of them and explains them in our ears.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For he alone more than supplies the place of all others to those who are specially eager for instruction.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I will only say this of him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Whenever I handle his Hexaemeron, and take its words on my lips, I am brought into the presence of the Creator, and understand the words of creation, and admire the Creator more than before, using my teacher as my only means of sight.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Whenever I take up his polemical works, I see the fire of Sodom, by which the wicked and rebellious tongues are reduced to ashes, or the tower of Chalane, impiously built, and righteously destroyed.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Whenever I read his writings on the Spirit, I find the God Whom I possess, and grow bold in my utterance of the truth, from the support of his theology and contemplation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His other treatises, in which he gives explanations for those who are shortsighted, by a threefold inscription on the solid tablets of his heart, lead me on from a mere literal or symbolical interpretation to a still wider view, as I proceed from one depth to another, calling upon deep after deep, and finding light after light, until I attain the highest pinnacle.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: When I study his panegyrics on our athletes, I despise the body, and enjoy the society of those whom he is praising, and rouse myself to the struggle.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His moral and practical discourses purify soul and body, making me a temple fit for God, and an instrument struck by the Spirit, to celebrate by its strains the glory and power of God.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In fact, he reduces me to harmony and order, and changes me by a Divine transformation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Since I have mentioned theology, and his most sublime treatises in this science, I will make this addition to what I have already said.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is of great service to the community, to save them from being injured by an unjustifiably low opinion of him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: My remarks are directed against those evil disposed persons who shelter their own vices under cover of their calumnies against others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In his defence of orthodox teaching, and of the union and coequal divinity of the Holy Trinity, to use terms which are, I think, as exact and clear as possible, he would have eagerly welcomed as a gain, and not a danger, not only expulsion from his see, in which he had originally no desire to be enthroned, but even exile, and death, and its preliminary tortures.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is manifest from his actual conduct and sufferings.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For when he had been sentenced to banishment on behalf of the truth, the only notice which he took of it was, to bid one of his servants to take his writing tablet and follow him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He held it necessary, according to the divine David’s advice, to guide his words with discretion, and to endure for a while the time of war, and the ascendency of the heretics, until it should be succeeded by a time of freedom and calm, which would admit of freedom of speech.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The enemy were on the watch for the unqualified statement “the Spirit is God;” which, although it is true, they and the wicked patron of their impiety imagined to be impious; so that they might banish him and his power of theological instruction from the city, and themselves be able to seize upon the church, and make it the starting point and citadel, from which they could overrun with their evil doctrine the rest of the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Accordingly, by the use of other terms, and by statements which unmistakably had the same meaning, and by arguments necessarily leading to this conclusion, he so overpowered his antagonists, that they were left without reply, and involved in their own admissions,—the greatest proof possible of dialectical power and skill.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His treatise on this subject makes it further manifest, being evidently written by a pen borrowed from the Spirit’s store.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He postponed for the time the use of the exact term, begging as a favour from the Spirit Himself and his earnest champions, that they would not be annoyed at his economy, nor, by clinging to a single expression, ruin the whole cause, from an uncompromising temper, at a crisis when religion was in peril.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He assured them that they would suffer no injury from a slight change in their expressions, and from teaching the same truth in other terms.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For our salvation is not so much a matter of words as of actions; for we would not reject the Jews, if they desired to unite with us, and yet for a while sought to use the term “Anointed” instead of “Christ:” while the community would suffer a very serious injury, if the church were seized upon by the heretics.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: That he, no less than any other, acknowledged that the Spirit is God, is plain from his often having publicly preached this truth, whenever opportunity offered, and eagerly confessed it when questioned in private.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But he made it more clear in his conversations with me, from whom he concealed nothing during our conferences upon this subject.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Not content with simply asserting it, he proceeded, as he had but very seldom done before, to imprecate upon himself that most terrible fate of separation from the Spirit, if he did not adore the Spirit as consubstantial and coequal with the Father and the Son.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if any one would accept me as having been his fellow labourer in this cause, I will set forth one point hitherto unknown to most men.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Under the pressure of the difficulties of the period, he himself undertook the economy, while allowing freedom of speech to me, whom no one was likely to drag from obscurity to trial or banishment, in order that by our united efforts our Gospel might be firmly established.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I mention this, not to defend his reputation, for the man is stronger than his assailants, if there are any such; but to prevent men from thinking that the terms found in his writings are the utmost limit of the truth, and so have their faith weakened, and consider that their own error is supported by his theology, which was the joint result of the influences of the time and of the Spirit, instead of considering the sense of his writings, and the object with which they were written, so as to be brought closer to the truth, and enabled to silence the partisans of impiety.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: At any rate let his theology be mine, and that of all dear to me!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And so confident am I of his spotlessness in this respect, that I take him for my partner in this, as in all else: and may what is mine be attributed to him, what is his to me, both at the hands of God, and of the wisest of men!

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For we would not say that the Evangelists are at variance with one another, because some are more occupied with the human side of the Christ, and others pay attention to His Divinity; some having commenced their history with what is within our own experience, others with what is above us; and by thus sharing the substance of their message, they have procured the advantage of those who receive it, and followed the impressions of the Spirit Who was within them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Come then, there have been many men of old days illustrious for piety, as lawgivers, generals, prophets, teachers, and men brave to the shedding of blood.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Let us compare our prelate with them, and thus recognize his merit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Adam was honoured by the hand of God, and the delights of Paradise, and the first legislation: but, unless I slander the reputation of our first parent, he kept not the command.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Now Basil both received and observed it, and received no injury from the tree of knowledge, and escaped the flaming sword, and, as I am well assured, has attained to Paradise.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Enos first ventured to call upon the Lord.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Basil both called upon Him himself, and, what is far more excellent, preached Him to others.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Enoch was translated, attaining to his translation as the reward of a little piety (for the faith was still in shadow) and escaped the peril of the remainder of life, but Basil’s whole life was a translation, and he was completely tested in a complete life.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Noah was entrusted with the ark, and the seeds of a new world committed to a small house of wood, in their preservation from the waters.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Basil escaped the deluge of impiety and made of his own city an ark of safety, which sailed lightly over the heretics, and afterwards recovered the whole world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Abraham was a great man, a patriarch, the offerer of the new sacrifice, by presenting to Him who had given it the promised seed, as a ready offering, eager for slaughter.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But Basil’s offering was no slight one, when he offered himself to God, without any equivalent being given in his stead, (for how could that have been possible?) so that his sacrifice was consummated.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Isaac was promised even before his birth, Basil promised himself, and took for his spouse Rebekah, I mean the Church, not fetched from a distance by the mission of a servant, but bestowed upon and entrusted to him by God close at home: nor was he outwitted in the preference of his children, but bestowed upon each what was due to him, without any deception, according to the judgment of the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I extol the ladder of Jacob, and the pillar which he anointed to God, and his wrestling with Him, whatever it was; and, in my opinion, it was the contrast and opposition of the human stature to the height of God, resulting in the tokens of the defeat of his race.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I extol also his clever devices and success in cattle-breeding, and his children, the twelve Patriarchs, and the distribution of his blessings, with their glorious prophecy of the future.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But I still more extol Basil for the ladder which he did not merely see, but which he ascended by successive steps towards excellence, and the pillar which he did not anoint, but which he erected to God, by pillorying the teaching of the ungodly; and the wrestling with which he wrestled, not with God, but, on behalf of God, to the overthrow of the heretics; and his pastoral care, whereby he grew rich, through gaining for himself a number of marked sheep greater than that of the unmarked, and his illustrious fruitfulness in spiritual children, and the blessing with which he established many.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Joseph was a provider of corn, but in Egypt only, and not frequently, and of bodily food.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Basil did so for all men, and at all times, and in spiritual food, and therefore, in my opinion, his was the more honourable function.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Like Job, the man of Uz, he was both tempted, and overcame, and at the close of his struggles gained splendid honour, having been shaken by none of his many assailants, and having gained a decisive victory over the efforts of the tempter, and put to silence the unreason of his friends, who knew not the mysterious character of his affliction.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: “Moses and Aaron among His priests.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Truly was Moses great, who inflicted the plagues upon Egypt, and delivered the people among many signs and wonders, and entered within the cloud, and sanctioned the double law, outward in the letter, and inward in the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Aaron was Moses’ brother, both naturally and spiritually, and offered sacrifices and prayers for the people, as the hierophant of the great and holy tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, and not man.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Of both of them Basil was a rival, for he tortured, not with bodily but with spiritual and mental plagues, the Egyptian race of heretics, and led to the land of promise the people of possession, zealous of good works; he inscribed laws, which are no longer obscure, but entirely spiritual, on tables which are not broken but are preserved; he entered the Holy of holies, not once a year, but often, I may say every day, and thence he revealed to us the Holy Trinity; and cleansed the people, not with temporary sprinklings, but with eternal purifications: What is the special excellence of Joshua?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: His generalship, and the distribution of the inheritance, and the taking possession of the Holy Land.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And was not Basil an Exarch?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Was he not a general of those who are saved by faith?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Did he not assign the different inheritances and abodes, according to the will of God, among his followers?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So that he too could use the words, “The lot is fallen unto me in pleasant places; and “my fortunes are in Thy hands,” fortunes more precious than those which come to us on earth, and can be snatched away.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Further, to run over the Judges, or the most illustrious of the Judges, there is “Samuel among those that call upon His Name,” who was given to God before his birth, and sanctified immediately after his birth, and the anointer with his horn of kings and priests.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But was not Basil as an infant consecrated to God from the womb, and offered with a coat at the altar, and was he not a seer of heavenly things, and anointed of the Lord, and the anointer of those who are perfected by the Spirit?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Among the kings, David is celebrated, whose victories and trophies gained from the enemy are on record, but his most characteristic trait was his gentleness, and, before his kingly office, his power with the harp, able to soothe even the evil spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Solomon asked of God and obtained breadth of heart, making the furthest possible progress in wisdom and contemplation, so that he became the most famous man of his time.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Basil, in my opinion, was in no wise, or but little inferior, to the one in gentleness, to the other in wisdom, so that he soothed the arrogance of infuriated sovereigns; and did not merely bring the queen of the south from the ends of the earth, or any other individual, to visit him because of his renown for wisdom, but made his wisdom known in all the ends of the world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I pass over the rest of Solomon’s life.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Even if we spare it, it is evident to all.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Do you praise the courage of Elijah in the presence of tyrants, and his fiery translation?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Or the fair inheritance of Elisha, the sheepskin mantle, accompanied by the spirit of Elijah?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: You must also praise the life of Basil, spent in the fire.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I mean in the multitude of temptations, and his escape through fire, which burnt, but did not consume, the mystery of “the bush,” and the fair cloak of skin from on high, his indifference to the flesh.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I pass by the rest, the three young men bedewed in the fire, the fugitive prophet praying in the whale’s belly, and coming forth from the creature, as from a chamber; the just man in the den, restraining the lions’ rage, and the struggle of the seven Maccabees, who were perfected with their father and mother in blood, and in all kinds of tortures.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Their endurance he rivalled, and won their glory.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I now turn to the New Testament, and comparing his life with those who are here illustrious, I shall find in the teachers a source of honour for their disciple.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Who was the forerunner of Jesus?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: John, the voice of the Word, the lamp of the Light, before Whom he even leaped in the womb, and Whom he preceded to Hades, whither he was despatched by the rage of Herod, to herald even there Him who was coming.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And, if my language seems audacious to anyone, let me assure him beforehand, that in making this comparison, I neither prefer Basil, nor imply that he is equal to him who surpasses all who are born of women, but only show that he was stirred to emulation, and possessed to some extent his striking features.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For it is no slight thing for the earnest to imitate the greatest of men, even in a slight degree.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Is it not indeed manifest that Basil was a copy of John’s asceticism?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He also lived in the wilderness, and wore in nightly watchings a ragged garb, during his shrinking retirement; he also loved a similar food, purifying himself for God by abstinence; he also was thought worthy to be a herald, if not a forerunner, of Christ, and there went out to him not only all the region round about, but also that which was beyond its borders; he also stood between the two covenants, abolishing the letter of the one by administering the spirit of the other, and bringing about the fulfilment of the hidden law through the dissolution of that which was apparent.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He emulated the zeal of Peter, the intensity of Paul, the faith of both these men of name and of surname, the lofty utterance of the sons of Zebedee, the frugality and simplicity of all the disciples.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Therefore he was also entrusted with the keys of the heavens, and not only from Jerusalem and round about unto Illyricum, but he embraces a wider circle in the Gospel; he is not named, but becomes, a Son of thunder; and lying upon the breast of Jesus, he draws thence the power of his word, and the depth of his thoughts.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He was prevented from becoming a Stephen, eager though he was, since reverence stayed the hands of those who would have stoned him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I am able to sum up still more concisely, to avoid treating in detail on these points of each individual.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In some respects he discovered, in some he emulated, in others he surpassed the good.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: In his many-sided virtues he excelled all men of this day.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: I have but one thing left to say, and in few words.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So great was his virtue, and the eminence of his fame, that many of his minor characteristics, nay, even his physical defects, have been assumed by others with a view to notoriety.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For instance his paleness, his beard, his gait, his thoughtful, and generally meditative, hesitation in speaking, which, in the ill-judged, inconsiderate imitation of many, took the form of melancholy.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And besides, the style of his dress, the shape of his bed, and his manner of eating, none of which was to him a matter of consequence, but simply the result of accident and chance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: So you might see many Basils in outward semblance, among these statues in outline, for it would be too much to call them his distant echo.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For an echo, though it is the dying away of a sound, at any rate represents it with great clearness, while these men fall too far short of him to satisfy even their desire to approach him.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Nor was it a slight thing, but a matter with good reason held in the highest estimation, to chance to have met him or done him some service, or to carry away the souvenir of something which he had said or done in jest or in earnest: as I know that I have myself often taken pride in doing; for his improvisations were much more precious and brilliant than the laboured efforts of other men.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But when, after he had finished his course, and kept the faith, he longed to depart, and the time for his crown was approaching, he did not hear the summons: “Get thee up into the mountain and die,” but “Die and come up to us.”

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And here again he wrought a wonder in no wise inferior to those mentioned before.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For when he was almost dead, and breathless, and had lost the greater part of his powers; he grew stronger in his last words, so as to depart with the utterances of religion, and, by ordaining the most excellent of his attendants, bestowed upon them both his hand and the Spirit: so that his disciples, who had aided him in his priestly office, might not be defrauded of the priesthood.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The remainder of my task I approach, but with reluctance, as it would fall more fully from the mouths of others than from my own.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: For I cannot philosophise over my misfortune, even if I greatly longed to do so, when I recollect that the loss is common to us all, and that the misfortune has befallen the whole world.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: He lay, drawing his last breath, and awaited by the choir on high, towards which he had long directed his gaze.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Around him poured the whole city, unable to bear his loss, inveighing against his departure, as if it had been an oppression, and clinging to his soul, as though it had been capable of restraint or compulsion at their hands or their prayers.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Their suffering had driven them distracted, all were eager, were it possible, to add to his life a portion of their own.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And when they failed, for it must needs be proved that he was a man, and, with his last words “Into thy Hands I commend my spirit,” he had joyfully resigned his soul to the care of the angels who carried him away; not without having some religious instructions and injunctions for the benefit of those who were present—then occurred a wonder more remarkable than any which had happened before.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The saint was being carried out, lifted high by the hands of holy men, and everyone was eager, some to seize the hem of his garment, others only just to touch the shadow, or the bier which bore his holy remains (for what could be more holy or pure than that body), others to draw near to those who were carrying it, others only to enjoy the sight, as if even this were beneficial.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Market places, porticos, houses of two or three stories were filled with people escorting, preceding, following, accompanying him, and trampling upon each other; tens of thousands of every race and age, beyond all previous experience.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The psalmody was overborne by the lamentations, philosophic resignation sank beneath the misfortune.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Our own people vied with strangers, Jews, Greeks, and foreigners, and they with us, for a greater share in the benefit, by means of a more abundant lamentation.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: To close my story, the calamity ended in danger; many souls departed along with him, from the violence of the pushing and confusion, who have been thought happy in their end, departing together with him, “funeral victims,” perhaps some fervid orator might call them.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: The body having at last escaped from those who would seize it, and made its way through those who went before it, was consigned to the tomb of his fathers, the high priest being added to the priests, the mighty voice which rings in my ears to the heralds, the martyr to the martyrs.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And now he is in heaven, where, if I mistake not, he is offering sacrifices for us, and praying for the people, for though, he has left us, he has not entirely left us.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: While I, Gregory, who am half dead, and, cleft in twain, torn away from our great union, and dragging along a life of pain which runs not easily, as may be supposed, after separation from him, know not what is to be my end now that I have lost my guidance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And even now I am admonished and instructed in nightly visions, if ever I fall short of my duty.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And my present object is not so much to mingle lamentations with my praises, or to portray the public life of the man, or publish a picture of virtue common to all time, and an example salutary to all churches, and to all souls, which we may keep in view, as a living law, and so rightly direct our lives as to counsel you, who have been completely initiated into his doctrine, to fix your eyes upon him, as one who sees you and is seen by you, and thus to be perfected by the Spirit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Come hither then, and surround me, all ye members of his choir, both of the clergy and the laity, both of our own country and from abroad; aid me in my eulogy, by each supplying or demanding the account of some of his excellences.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Regard, ye occupants of the bench, the lawgiver; ye politicians, the statesman; ye men of the people, his orderliness; ye men of letters, the instructor; ye virgins, the leader of the bride; ye who are yoked in marriage, the restrainer; ye hermits, him who gave you wings; ye cenobites, the judge; ye simple men, the guide; ye contemplatives, the divine; ye cheerful ones, the bridle; ye unfortunate men, the consoler, the staff of hoar hairs, the guide of youth, the relief of poverty, the steward of abundance.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Widows also will, I imagine, praise their protector, orphans their father, poor men their friend, strangers their entertainer, brothers the man of brotherly love, the sick their physician, whatever be their sickness and the healing they need, the healthy the preserver of health, and all men him who made himself all things to all that he might gain the majority, if not all.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: This is my offering to thee, Basil, uttered by the tongue which once was the sweetest of all to thee, of him who was thy fellow in age and rank.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: If it have approached thy deserts, thanks are due to thee, for it was from confidence in thee that I undertook to speak of thee.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: But if it fall far short of thy expectations, what must be our feelings, who are worn out with age and disease and regret for thee?

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yet God is pleased, when we do what we can.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Yet mayest thou gaze upon us from above, thou divine and sacred person; either stay by thy entreaties our thorn in the flesh, given to us by God for our discipline, or prevail upon us to bear it boldly, and guide all our life towards that which is most for our profit.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: And if we be translated, do thou receive us there also in thine own tabernacle, that, as we dwell together, and gaze together more clearly and more perfectly upon the holy and blessed Trinity, of Which we have now in some degree received the image, our longing may at last be satisfied, by gaining this recompense for all the battles we have fought and the assaults we have endured.

verses.book: Select Orations of Saint Gregory Nazianzen.
verses.text: Such are our words on thy behalf: who will there be to praise us, since we leave this life after thee, even if we offer any topic worthy of words or praise in Christ Jesus our Lord, to Whom be glory forever?
